frame_label: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
generated_comment:
A: Mom, these days in Mumbai also you can't be too careless, yaar.
B: Haan beta, girl should be sensible only — late night ghoomna, cab alone, random parties... why take risk when zamaana hi aisa hai?
A: Exactly, I'm not blaming anyone, but practically speaking women have to be extra careful.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women's safety as their personal responsibility and normalizes male violence as an unavoidable feature of 'zamaana.' It uses protective, practical language to justify restricting women's movement rather than challenging predatory behavior.
original_frame: Female Education as Threat, Waste, or Conditional Privilege
persona_text: You are Rohit. Age 28. Single. A man in Mumbai. You are on a matrimonial website talking to families for yourself. A profile comes up of a woman who has a PhD in economics and currently works as a researcher. She is 27 and by every measure an exceptional match. You are telling your mother about this profile. Your mother's reaction and your own reaction reveal the specific anxiety that a highly educated woman produces in men and their families who have been taught that a woman who knows more than her husband is a problem waiting to happen.
